concerns.text: Task T021 and T022 implement Neff and p-value calculation on the 'full continuous series' as per FR-010, but there is NO task to calculate the effective sample size (Neff) or autocorrelation-adjusted p-values specifically for the TEST SET (2018-2020) required by SC-003. SC-003 mandates: 'The multiple-testing correction is recomputed on the validation set using the same Bonferroni procedure applied to p-values that have been adjusted for autocorrelation via the effective-sample-size method (applied to the full validation period data).' Without a task to compute Neff for the 2018-2020 subset, the validation report cannot satisfy SC-003.

Task T032 states 'calculate Test set correlations and compare against global threshold', but SC-003 requires the validation set to have its OWN autocorrelation-adjusted p-values and Bonferroni correction recomputed. There is no task to implement the Neff calculation and Bonferroni correction logic specifically for the 2018-2020 test window. The current tasks only cover the global (1998-2020) calculation in Phase 4.

Task T012 implements abort logic for missing variables, but FR-006 explicitly lists the exact variable names ('N_p', 'T_p', 'He2+_ratio') and SC-002 requires an explicit error message logging the missing variable name. Task T012 description is generic ('abort with clear error') and does not explicitly mandate logging the specific missing variable name as required by SC-002. A more specific task description or a separate task is needed to ensure SC-002 compliance.

Task T021 references 'Pyper & Peterman method' but does not explicitly cite the formula or library function to be used, which creates ambiguity for implementation. While FR-010 mentions the method, the task description should be more specific (e.g., 'implement lag-1 autocorrelation estimator using scipy.stats') to ensure the 'Verified Accuracy' principle is met during implementation, preventing interpretation drift.
